title: T-041 — D365 outbox dispatcher + JournalLines anti-corruption adapter (08-PROD-e, §8.2.7 / §12)
prompt:
# T-041 — D365 outbox dispatcher + JournalLines anti-corruption adapter

PRD: docs/prd/08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §8.2.7, §6 D8, §7.5 (Epic E5 INTEGRATIONS stage 2), §5.1#7 (R1 event-first)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement the async outbox dispatcher job that drains `production_outbox_events` (created in T-009), maps WO completion + output + waste rows into D365 JournalLines via a dedicated R15 anti-corruption adapter, marks events delivered on success, and moves exhausted failures into `d365_push_dlq` (T-010). D365 must never become the source-of-truth for factory release or WO state.

## Implementation contract
1. `packages/api-services/production/d365-journallines-adapter.ts` exports `mapWoConfirmationToJournalLines(canonical)` — pure mapper from internal model (GS1-first identifiers) to D365 LEGACY-D365 payload per §6 D8.
2. `packages/api-services/production/d365-outbox-dispatcher.ts` reads pending events, applies the adapter, calls the D365 HTTP client, on success sets `status='delivered'`, on failure increments `attempt_count` and schedules next retry per §5.4 (5min/30min/2h/12h/24h); on 5 failures inserts a `d365_push_dlq` row with raw canonical payload + mapped payload + last error.
3. `apps/web/app/api/internal/jobs/production/d365-dispatch/route.ts` Vercel cron entrypoint runs the dispatcher every 30s with batch_size from settings.
4. Dispatcher respects `integration.d365.push.enabled` and `integration.d365.push.dry_run` flags read from 02-SETTINGS; dry_run logs but never calls the D365 API and never marks delivered.
5. Delivery state mutations only touch outbox/DLQ rows; they must not write to `wo_executions`, `active_bom_header_id`, `active_factory_spec_id`, or any release row.

## Files
- packages/api-services/production/d365-outbox-dispatcher.ts [create]
- packages/api-services/production/d365-journallines-adapter.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/internal/jobs/production/d365-dispatch/route.ts [create]
- packages/api-services/production/__tests__/d365-outbox-dispatcher.test.ts [create]
- packages/api-services/production/__tests__/d365-journallines-adapter.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a pending `production_outbox_events` row of type `wo.completed` plus its output/waste rows, when dispatcher runs and the D365 client returns 200, then outbox row `status='delivered'`, `delivered_at` is set, and a JournalLines payload matching §6 D8 shape was sent.
2. Given the D365 client fails 5 times with retriable errors, when dispatcher runs the final attempt, then a `d365_push_dlq` row is created with raw canonical payload, mapped payload, last error, and the outbox row is marked `status='failed'`.
3. Given dispatcher success or failure, when post-condition is inspected, then no `wo_executions`, factory release, `active_bom_header_id`, or `active_factory_spec_id` row was modified.

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/api-services/production/__tests__/d365-outbox-dispatcher.test.ts + d365-journallines-adapter.test.ts asserting the three ACs and dry_run no-op before the dispatcher/adapter exist.
- Run `pnpm vitest run packages/api-services/production/__tests__/d365-outbox-dispatcher.test.ts packages/api-services/production/__tests__/d365-journallines-adapter.test.ts`
- Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not let D365 push success/failure mutate canonical release status, WO START eligibility, `active_bom_header_id`, or `active_factory_spec_id`.
- Do not bypass the outbox; mutations must enqueue not call D365 inline.
- Do not store D365 credentials in this module — read via 02-SETTINGS §11 secrets adapter.
- Do not delete DLQ rows on resolve (T-042 owns audit-preserving mark-resolved).

pipeline_inputs.description: Async outbox dispatcher draining production_outbox_events into D365 JournalLines via R15 anti-corruption adapter, with retry schedule and DLQ creation; D365 stays side-effect only.
pipeline_inputs.details: Dispatcher runs from Vercel cron (every 30s) with batch size from settings. Adapter is a pure function so it's covered by table-driven tests. Retry schedule per §5.4 (5/30min/2h/12h/24h). Dry_run logs but never calls the D365 API and never marks delivered. Credentials/feature-flags read via 02-SETTINGS §11. DLQ insertion is audit-preserving: keeps raw canonical, mapped payload, last_error, attempt_count; T-042 owns replay/resolve.
out_of_scope: Do not implement DLQ list/replay/resolve API (T-042)
Do not implement DLQ admin UI (T-051)
Do not emit outbox events from runtime endpoints in this task (handled in T-016..T-034)
acceptance_criteria: Given a pending production_outbox_events row of type wo.completed, when dispatcher runs and D365 returns 200, then outbox row status='delivered', delivered_at set, and JournalLines payload matches §6 D8 shape.

Given the D365 client fails 5 retriable times, when dispatcher runs the final attempt, then a d365_push_dlq row is created with raw canonical, mapped payload and last error, and outbox row status='failed'.
Given dispatcher success or failure, when post-condition is inspected, then no wo_executions/release/active_bom_header_id/active_factory_spec_id row was modified.
test_strategy: RED: write d365-outbox-dispatcher.test.ts + d365-journallines-adapter.test.ts asserting status matrix, retry schedule, DLQ creation, dry_run no-op and release-state non-mutation before code exists.
risk_red_lines: Do not let D365 push success/failure mutate canonical release status, WO START eligibility, active_bom_header_id, or active_factory_spec_id.
Do not bypass the outbox (no synchronous inline D365 calls per §5.1#7).
Do not store D365 credentials in this module.
Do not delete DLQ rows on resolve.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: D365 connection config + push feature flags + dry_run toggle.
